title: calamus ashtonii
intro: Calamus ashtonii, also known as the Wild Ginger, is a type of plant native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The rhizomes and roots of the Calamus ashtonii plant possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The health benefits of Calamus ashtonii include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and vomiting, and reduce inflammation and pain. Calamus ashtonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a versatile and useful addition to natural remedies.
